items.title: You have to look at how quick OKC is making a decision to kick as soon as they see Wembanyama pull in to the paint. Spurs have always worked to rotate around him but it's going to be key in this one.

items.title: Dear Thunder fans, you don't get to boo that Wemby move to draw a foul when your team employs SGA. Thank you for your attention to this matter.
items.author: Kurt Helin
items.body_excerpt: Dear Thunder fans, you don't get to boo that Wemby move to draw a foul when your team employs SGA. Thank you for your attention to this matter.

items.title: OKC looking to drive but looking to use those drives to poke at the Spurs rotations and Wemby. Wallace drives vs. Barnes, Wembanyama pulls into the paint to help. Skip pass is there to Caruso, rotation is there. Extra to Williams for 3.
items.author: Steve Jones
items.body_excerpt: OKC looking to drive but looking to use those drives to poke at the Spurs rotations and Wemby. Wallace drives vs. Barnes, Wembanyama pulls into the paint to help. Skip pass is there to Caruso, rotation is there. Extra to Williams for 3.

items.title: Caitlin Clark finally got her first Indy 500 experience — serving as Grand Marshal in front of more than 330,000 fans.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Caitlin Clark finally got her first Indy 500 experience — serving as Grand Marshal in front of more than 330,000 fans.

She wasn’t alone: Hull, White and more than half the Pacers roster witnessed the closest finish in race history.

Story + video: www.fieldhousefiles.com/p/caitlin-cl...

items.title: After a rough shooting Game 4, the Thunder bench is 5-of-8 to start Game 5.
items.author: Kurt Helin
items.body_excerpt: After a rough shooting Game 4, the Thunder bench is 5-of-8 to start Game 5.

items.title: OKC poking at the Spurs defense and playing out of breakdowns and Wemby's rotations. Chet screens and the Spurs switch that. Chet works to seal. Advance to McCain who gets a screen from Williams. No switch, roll is there and Wemby helps from strong side corner. Open 3 there.
items.author: Steve Jones
items.body_excerpt: OKC poking at the Spurs defense and playing out of breakdowns and Wemby's rotations. Chet screens and the Spurs switch that. Chet works to seal. Advance to McCain who gets a screen from Williams. No switch, roll is there and Wemby helps from strong side corner. Open 3 there.

items.title: All four Thunderbuddy reserves have made a 3
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
All four Thunderbuddy reserves have made a 3

The Thunder bench (including McCain) made only five 3s in all of Game 4, with two of them coming in a non-competitive fourth quarter.

items.title: Hollinger POTD Mehmet Okur. Turkish big was drafted 37th by DET in 01. Came to NBA in 02 & was good bench guy but annoyed Larry Brown. Signed big deal w/UTA in 04, scored 18 ppg & was all-star. Great stretch big but weak on D. In all, a very good player.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollinger POTD Mehmet Okur. Turkish big was drafted 37th by DET in 01. Came to NBA in 02 & was good bench guy but annoyed Larry Brown. Signed big deal w/UTA in 04, scored 18 ppg & was all-star. Great stretch big but weak on D. In all, a very good player.

items.title: Spurs outscored Thunder 11-6 off turnovers in that first quarter. Thunder allowed the 2nd-fewest points off turnovers allowed in regular season (14.7, only Boston allowed fewer)
items.author: Law Murray 📘
items.body_excerpt:
Spurs outscored Thunder 11-6 off turnovers in that first quarter. Thunder allowed the 2nd-fewest points off turnovers allowed in regular season (14.7, only Boston allowed fewer)

Oklahoma City did even that out by outscoring Spurs 7-2 on 2nd-chance points.

items.title: Julian Champagnie:
items.author: Nate Duncan
items.body_excerpt:
Julian Champagnie:


Regular-season points per GAME: 11


First-quarter points tonight: 13


The only other player with such a low scoring average to score so much in a conference finals or NBA Finals first quarter (play-by-play era) is Meyers Leonard in 2019.

items.title: Just another reminder that Cason Wallace led the league in steals. You can't dribble haphazardly around him. Dylan Harper had to find out.
